danger_signals.visual_form: Animated hostile sprites with distinct color palettes; sprite tile variations for different enemy types
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Lava, acid, and quicksand depicted through environmental tileset changes; water/fog visual layers
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental damage; navigable only with specific suit upgrades or abilities

danger_signals.visual_form: Unique room architecture; large sprite allocations; specific enemy set loading
Sealed doors, barriers, crumble blocks, Zebetite barriers (pink/purple geometric patterns)
danger_signals.meaning: Area inaccessible without specific power-up or weapon combination

danger_signals.visual_form: HUD health bar visually depletes; Samus animation changes to hunched/damaged pose

safety_signals.visual_form: Fixed architectural structure; glowing visual indicator; specific room design
safety_signals.meaning: Safe point; progression checkpoint; health/ammo restoration

safety_signals.visual_form: Large metallic vessel sprite at predefined locations; distinctive yellow/orange hull
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; save/restore point; narrative anchor

safety_signals.visual_form: Floating power-up sprites with glow effect; inventory screen visual confirmation
safety_signals.meaning: Capability expansion; new traversal or combat option unlocked

safety_signals.visual_form: Small floating orbs (green for energy, red for missiles); visual absorption animation
safety_signals.meaning: Health/ammo restoration; safety achieved through collection

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual flash/glow effect when Reserve Tank activates; HUD indicator change
safety_signals.meaning: Secondary safety net; health replenishment from backup system

faction_indicators.color_identity: Purple/dark teal palette for enemy sprites and associated rooms
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Enemy sprite designs; architectural style of pirate-controlled areas
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types with pirate-associated appearance; upgraded variants (e.g., Torizo variants)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Crashed Ship tileset; specific room layouts; purple/dark atmospheric coloring in Space Pirate territory

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Chozo Statues as environmental fixtures; Chozo iconography in room design
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Torizo bosses (golden armored Chozo); statue-based enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Brinstar tileset; gold/blue color scheme; temple-like architecture; Chozo artifact rooms with distinct visual presentation

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red/orange danger coloring; alien biomorphic aesthetics
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Metroid creature sprite; larva form; mature Super Metroid form
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Larval Metroid (small); mature Super Metroid (large, powerful); Metroid Prime (regional boss variant visual)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tourian tileset; red/organic coloring; breeding chamber aesthetics; Metroid hive areas

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ridley boss sprite; unique animation set distinguishing from generic pirates
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard Ridley form; Golden Torizo as parallel threat; Ridley appears across multiple boss encounters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Crateria leadership areas; Ridley's lair aesthetics; boss room distinctive architecture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darkened tileset; inactive machinery sprites; collapsed structural elements; dim lighting palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced enemy spawning; environmental hazards inactive; rooms inaccessible due to collapsed passages

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Brightened tileset; active machinery animation; restored structural integrity visuals; elevated lighting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Full enemy encounters enabled; environmental hazards active; previously blocked passages open

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Automap reveals room layout; map display updates HUD; unexplored areas show as blank/unknown
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Navigation clarity increases; minimap provides tactical information; saves persist map discovery

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Boss sprite removal; environmental state change (e.g., barriers dissolve, pathways open); visual confirmation animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Doors unlock; new areas become accessible; enemy spawning patterns change; progression enables

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unique tileset; organic alien architecture; pulsating environmental color shifts; Metroid transformation sequence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hyper Beam acquisition triggers visual/gameplay transformation; post-game ending state locked

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ending slide shows Samus unarmored/posed differently; visual reward variation based on time
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multiple ending states; post-game cutscene variant; Dachora/Etecoon rescue rescue state affects ending visuals

environmental_storytelling.example: Crateria (orange/tan, rocky terrain) vs. Norfair (red/lava, volcanic) vs. Brinstar (blue/overgrown, jungle) communicate distinct planetary regions and ecological zones without explicit narration
Aquatic enemies in flooded areas; fire-resistant creatures in lava zones; Chozo-affiliated enemies in temple areas; pirate-aligned enemies in industrial/crashed ship zones
High-tier items placed in dangerous boss rooms or well-guarded vaults suggest scarcity and value; redundant power-ups in accessible areas suggest abundance
Wrecked Ship's deterioration (offline state) visually communicates abandonment; Crateria's ruined structures suggest ancient conflict
Chozo statues as navigational landmarks and lore anchors; golden statues indicate Chozo presence; broken/damaged statues suggest fallen civilization
Lava/acid tileset visual distinction; water overlay effects; quicksand animation; each hazard's appearance communicates traversal requirements
Futuristic save station design vs. organic Chozo architecture communicates technological layering of world; placement near danger suggests intentional strategic positioning

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Sprite orientation follows input direction; running animation; jumping animation with apex control
Sprite crouches; vertical hitbox reduced; enables corner-hugging; visual signal of precise positioning mode
Sprite transforms into ball; enables bomb jumps; visual state change signals new capability set
Visual energy buildup animation; weapon sprite glows intensifying; audio cue accompanies
Sprite swings on beam line; momentum-based animation; visual rope/beam connection
Looping patrol pattern; sprite animation looped; predictable positioning
Animation speed increases; sprite orientation focuses on player; attack pattern initiates
Sprite removes from screen; death animation plays; item drop animation if applicable; enemy RAM slot freed
Predictable attack patterns; sprite size larger; room-dominating presence; limited special moves
Attack frequency increases; new attack patterns unlock; sprite color shift or damage animation intensifies; desperation behavior
Golden sprite coloration; enhanced durability; new attack patterns; indicates upgraded threat level
Sprite blocks passage; no interaction possible; visual barrier indicates progression requirement
Sprite opens/becomes passable; interaction enabled; visual state change communicates progression
Visual glow/activation animation; Samus enters interaction state; save menu appears
Sprite floats with bobbing animation; glow effect; visual draw communicates value
Absorption animation; sprite vanishes; inventory HUD updates; visual feedback confirms acquisition

notes: Super Metroid establishes manifestation patterns primarily through tileset-based environmental communication, sprite-color faction marking, and behavioral animation tells. The game lacks explicit UI for faction relationships but communicates world state through architecture, enemy type placement, and hazard visualization. The automap system provides meta-layer world-state communication. No explicit political system exists; world state is communicated through area accessibility (locked by power-ups) and enemy spawn behavior (tied to regional control). Samus's animation hitbox variations enable advanced traversal mechanics but remain transparent to casual players—a foundational design principle for the metroidvania genre.
